Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. Dark Angel is an American thrash metal band from Downey, California, that formed in 1981. The band's current lineup includes drummer Gene Hoglan, guitarists Eric Meyer and Laura Christine, frontman Ron Rinehart and bassist Mike Gonzalez. In its history, they have gone through many lineup changes, and as of guitarist Jim Durkin's death in March 2023, there are no original members left in the current lineup of Dark Angel. Meyer, who joined in 1984, is the only member to appear on all of their albums. Dark Angel's over-the-top style (extremely fast, heavy and lengthy songs with many tempo changes, lyrics, and extended instrumental parts) earned them the nickname "The L.A. Caffeine Machine". Despite never achieving mainstream success, the band is often credited as one of the leaders of
B. In 1981, Dark Angel, an American thrash metal band from Downey, California, formed. Their current lineup includes drummer Gene Hoglan, guitarists Eric Meyer and Laura Christine, frontman Ron Rinehart, and bassist Mike Gonzalez. They have undergone numerous lineup changes over the years, with no original members remaining after the death of guitarist Jim Durkin in March 2023. Meyer, who joined in 1984, is the only member who has appeared on all of their albums. The band was known as "The L.A. Caffeine Machine Machines".
C. Dark Angel is a thrash metal group from the United States, originating in Downey, California, and established in 1981. The ensemble currently consists of Gene Hoglan on drums, Eric Meyer and Laura Christine on guitars, Ron Rinehart as the lead vocalist, and Mike Gonzalez on bass. Throughout its existence, the band has experienced numerous shifts in personnel. Following the passing of guitarist Jim Durkin in March 2023, no founding members remain in Dark Angel's present configuration. Meyer, who became a member in 1984, is the sole individual to have participated in the recording of all the band's albums. Dark Angel's distinctive and exaggerated approach – characterized by exceptionally rapid, intense, and extended compositions featuring frequent alterations in tempo, intricate lyrical content, and expansive instrumental sections – led to them being dubbed "The L.A. Caffeine Machine." Although the band never attained widespread commercial recognition, they are frequently acknowledged as influential pioneers within the genre.
